query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 55bab3d5).

## Issue to Resolve

Do not merge annotated expression with function signature rule
Given code below:
```
private fun getErrorMessage(value: String): String =
        @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
        customMessage.takeUnless { it.isEmpty() } ?: String.format(DEFAULT_ERROR_MESSAGE_WITH_NO_REASON, value)
```
*and* assume that the `function-signature` rule is enabled *and* the (first line of the) function body expression has to be merged with the function signature.

For example with `.editorconfig`:
```
[{*.kt,*.kts}]
ktlint_code_style = android
ktlint_standard=enabled
ktlint_experimental=enabled
```
the command `ktlint --relative` results in
```
17:41:39.417 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
x.kts:6:97: Unnecessary trailing comma before ")" (standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:1:53: First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature (standard:function-signature)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:1: Exceeded max line length (100) (standard:max-line-length)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:31: Missing newline after "{" (standard:wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:46: Expected newline (standard:annotation)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:64: Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line) (standard:argument-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:102: Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line) (standard:argument-list-wrapping)
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:3:107: Missing newline before ")" (standard:argument-list-wrapping)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  standard:argument-list-wrapping: 3
  standard:annotation: 1
  standard:function-signature: 1
  standard:max-line-length: 1
  standard:trailing-comma-on-call-site: 1
  standard:wrapping: 1
```
When running `format` the output changes to:
```
17:44:18.369 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt:1:53: Expected newline before annotation (standard:annotation)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  standard:annotation: 1
```
This log message is rather confusing when you compare it with the original code sample. However, it should not be compared with the original but with the modified code to make sense.
and the file is formatted as:
```
private fun getErrorMessage(value: String): String = @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
customMessage.takeUnless { it.isEmpty() }
    ?: String.format(DEFAULT_ERROR_MESSAGE_WITH_NO_REASON, value)
```

The `function-signature` should not format in such a way that if violates the `annotation` rule.

_Originally posted by @paul-dingemans in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2028#issuecomment-1551678889_

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

124â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
125â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
126â†’            )
127â†’    }
128â†’
129â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
130â†’        val funNode =
131â†’            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
132â†’                this.treeParent
133â†’            } else {
134â†’                this
135â†’            }
136â†’        funNode
137â†’            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
138â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
139â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
140â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
141â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
142â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
143â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
144â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
145â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
146â†’                    ) {
147â†’                        return currentNode
148â†’                    }
149â†’                }
150â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
151â†’            }
152â†’        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
153â†’    }
154â†’
155â†’    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
156â†’        node: ASTNode,
157â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
158â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
159â†’    ) {
160â†’        require(node.elementType == FUN)
161â†’
162â†’        val forceMultilineSignature =
163â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
164â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
165â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
166â†’        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
167â†’            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
168â†’            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
169â†’            // like below, which are not acceptable:
170â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
171â†’            //     ) = "some-value"
172â†’            //
173â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
174â†’            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
175â†’            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
176â†’            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
177â†’            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
178â†’            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
179â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
180â†’                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
181â†’                // signature needs to be recalculated
182â†’                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
183â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
184â†’            } else {
185â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
186â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
187â†’            }
188â†’        } else {
189â†’            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
190â†’            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
191â†’            // signature.
192â†’            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
193â†’                node
194â†’                    .functionSignatureNodes()
195â†’                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
196â†’            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
197â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
198â†’            } else {
199â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
200â†’            }
201â†’        }
202â†’    }
203â†’
204â†’    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
205â†’        val closingParenthesis =
206â†’            node
207â†’                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
208â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
209â†’        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
210â†’            node
211â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
212â†’                .childrenBetween(
213â†’                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
214â†’                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
215â†’                )
216â†’
217â†’        return node.indent(false).length +
218â†’            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
219â†’    }
220â†’
221â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
222â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
223â†’            ?.children()
224â†’            .orEmpty()
225â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
226â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
227â†’
228â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
229â†’        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
230â†’            ?.children()
231â†’            .orEmpty()
232â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
233â†’            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
234â†’
235â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
236â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
237â†’            ?.children()
238â†’            .orEmpty()
239â†’            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
240â†’
241â†’    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
242â†’        node: ASTNode,
243â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
244â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
247â†’
248â†’        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
249â†’        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
250â†’        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
251â†’            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
252â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
253â†’    }
254â†’
255â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
256â†’
257â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
258â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
259â†’            .joinTextToString()
260â†’            .length
261â†’
262â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
263â†’        node: ASTNode,
264â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
265â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
266â†’        multiline: Boolean,
267â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
268â†’    ): Int {
269â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
270â†’
271â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
272â†’        val firstParameterInList =
273â†’            valueParameterList
274â†’                .children()
275â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
276â†’
277â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
278â†’            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
279â†’                // handle empty parameter list
280â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
281â†’            } else {
282â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
283â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
285â†’            }
286â†’
287â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
288â†’    }
289â†’
290â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
291â†’        node: ASTNode,
292â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
293â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
294â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
295â†’    ): Int {
296â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
297â†’
298â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
299â†’
300â†’        valueParameterList
301â†’            .children()
302â†’            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
303â†’            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
304â†’                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
305â†’                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
306â†’                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
307â†’            }
308â†’            .firstOrNull()
309â†’            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
310â†’                if (!dryRun) {
311â†’                    emit(
312â†’                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
313â†’                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
314â†’                        true,
315â†’                    )
316â†’                }
317â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
318â†’                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
319â†’                } else {
320â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
321â†’                }
322â†’            }
323â†’
324â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
325â†’    }
326â†’
327â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
328â†’        node: ASTNode,
329â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
330â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
331â†’        multiline: Boolean,
332â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
333â†’    ): Int {
334â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
335â†’
336â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
337â†’        val firstParameterInList =
338â†’            valueParameterList
339â†’                .children()
340â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
341â†’
342â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
343â†’        firstParameter
344â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
345â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
346â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
347â†’                if (multiline) {
348â†’                    val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
349â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
350â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
351â†’                    ) {
352â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
353â†’                            emit(
354â†’                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
355â†’                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
356â†’                                true,
357â†’                            )
358â†’                        }
359â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361â†’                        } else {
362â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363â†’                        }
364â†’                    }
365â†’                } else {
366â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
367â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
368â†’                            emit(
369â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
370â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
371â†’                                true,
372â†’                            )
373â†’                        }
374â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
375â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
376â†’                        } else {
377â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
378â†’                        }
379â†’                    }
380â†’                }
381â†’            }
382â†’
383â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
384â†’    }
385â†’
386â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
387â†’        node: ASTNode,
388â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
389â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
390â†’        multiline: Boolean,
391â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
392â†’    ): Int {
393â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
394â†’
395â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
396â†’        val firstParameterInList =
397â†’            valueParameterList
398â†’                .children()
399â†’                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
400â†’
401â†’        valueParameterList
402â†’            .children()
403â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
404â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
405â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->
406â†’                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
407â†’                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
408â†’                    ?.prevLeaf()
409â†’                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
410â†’                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
411â†’                        if (multiline) {
412â†’                            val expectedParameterIndent = node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent)
413â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
414â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
415â†’                            ) {
416â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
417â†’                                    emit(
418â†’                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
419â†’                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
420â†’                                        true,
421â†’                                    )
422â†’                                }
423â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
424â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
425â†’                                } else {
426â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
427â†’                                }
428â†’                            }
429â†’                        } else {
430â†’                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
431â†’                                if (!dryRun) {
432â†’                                    emit(
433â†’                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
434â†’                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
435â†’                                        true,
436â†’                                    )
437â†’                                }
438â†’                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
439â†’                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
440â†’                                } else {
441â†’                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
442â†’                                }
443â†’                            }
444â†’                        }
445â†’                    }
446â†’            }
447â†’
448â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
449â†’    }
450â†’
451â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
452â†’        node: ASTNode,
453â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
454â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
455â†’        multiline: Boolean,
456â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
457â†’    ): Int {
458â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
459â†’
460â†’        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
461â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
462â†’
463â†’        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
464â†’        closingParenthesis
465â†’            ?.prevSibling()
466â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
467â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
468â†’                if (multiline) {
469â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
470â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
471â†’                    ) {
472â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
473â†’                            emit(
474â†’                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
475â†’                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
476â†’                                true,
477â†’                            )
478â†’                        }
479â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
480â†’                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
481â†’                        } else {
482â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
483â†’                        }
484â†’                    }
485â†’                } else {
486â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
487â†’                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
488â†’                    ) {
489â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
490â†’                            emit(
491â†’                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
492â†’                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
493â†’                                true,
494â†’                            )
495â†’                        }
496â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
497â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
498â†’                        } else {
499â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
500â†’                        }
501â†’                    }
502â†’                }
503â†’            }
504â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
505â†’    }

507â†’    private fun fixFunctionBody(
508â†’        node: ASTNode,
509â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
510â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
511â†’        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
512â†’    ) {
513â†’        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
514â†’            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
515â†’        } else {
516â†’            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
517â†’        }
518â†’    }
519â†’
520â†’    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
521â†’        node: ASTNode,
522â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
523â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
524â†’        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
525â†’    ) {
526â†’        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
527â†’            node
528â†’                .findChildByType(EQ)
529â†’                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
530â†’                ?: return
531â†’        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
532â†’        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
533â†’        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
534â†’
535â†’        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
536â†’            functionBodyExpressionNodes
537â†’                .joinTextToString()
538â†’                .split("\n")
539â†’        functionBodyExpressionLines
540â†’            .firstOrNull()
541â†’            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
542â†’                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
543â†’                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
544â†’                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
545â†’                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
546â†’                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
547â†’                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
548â†’                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
549â†’                                )
550â†’                        } else {
551â†’                            false
552â†’                        }
553â†’                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
554â†’                        emit(
555â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
556â†’                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
557â†’                            true,
558â†’                        )
559â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
560â†’                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
561â†’                        }
562â†’                    }
563â†’                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
564â†’                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
565â†’                ) {
566â†’                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
567â†’                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
568â†’                    ) {
569â†’                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
570â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
571â†’                        ) {
572â†’                            emit(
573â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
574â†’                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
575â†’                                true,
576â†’                            )
577â†’                            if (autoCorrect) {
578â†’                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
579â†’                                    .first()
580â†’                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
581â†’                            }
582â†’                        }
583â†’                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
584â†’                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
585â†’                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
586â†’                    ) {
587â†’                        emit(
588â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
589â†’                            "Newline expected before expression body",
590â†’                            true,
591â†’                        )
592â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
593â†’                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
594â†’                                .first()
595â†’                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
596â†’                        }
597â†’                    }
598â†’                }
599â†’            }
600â†’    }
601â†’
602â†’    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
603â†’        functionSignatureNodes()
604â†’            .childrenBetween(
605â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
606â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
607â†’            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
608â†’            .split("\n")
609â†’            .lastOrNull()
610â†’            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
611â†’            ?: false
612â†’
613â†’    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
614â†’        node: ASTNode,
615â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
616â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
617â†’    ) {
618â†’        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
619â†’            node
620â†’                .getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody()
621â†’                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
622â†’                ?: return
623â†’
624â†’        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
625â†’        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
626â†’        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
627â†’
628â†’        functionBodyBlock
629â†’            .joinTextToString()
630â†’            .split("\n")
631â†’            .firstOrNull()
632â†’            ?.also {
633â†’                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
634â†’                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
635â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
636â†’                        functionBodyBlock
637â†’                            .first()
638â†’                            .prevLeaf(true)
639â†’                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
640â†’                    }
641â†’                }
642â†’            }
643â†’    }

645â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
646â†’        this
647â†’            .collectLeavesRecursively()
648â†’            .childrenBetween(
649â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
650â†’                endASTNodePredicate = {
651â†’                    // collect all remaining nodes
652â†’                    false
653â†’                },
654â†’            )

656â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull() =
657â†’        this
658â†’            .firstOrNull()
659â†’            ?.takeIf { first -> first.isWhiteSpace() }

661â†’    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF

663â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }

665â†’    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
666â†’        if (psi is LeafElement) {
667â†’            listOf(this)
668â†’        } else {
669â†’            children()
670â†’                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
671â†’                .toList()
672â†’        }

674â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
675â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
676â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
677â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
678â†’        val iterator = iterator()
679â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
680â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
681â†’
682â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
683â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
684â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
685â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
686â†’                break
687â†’            }
688â†’        }
689â†’
690â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
692â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
693â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694â†’                break
695â†’            }
696â†’        }
697â†’
698â†’        return childrenBetween
699â†’    }
700â†’
701â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
702â†’        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
703â†’
704â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
705â†’
706â†’    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
707â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
708â†’        return valueParameterList
709â†’            .children()
710â†’            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
711â†’    }
712â†’
713â†’    private fun ASTNode.getLastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody(): ASTNode? =
714â†’        this
715â†’            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
716â†’            ?.firstChildNode
717â†’            ?.prevCodeLeaf()
718â†’
719â†’    public companion object {

726â†’                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +

728â†’                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
729â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
730â†’                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
731â†’                    ),
732â†’                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
733â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
734â†’                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
735â†’                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {
736â†’                        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET
737â†’                    } else {

739â†’                    }

742â†’                    if (property == FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET) {

746â†’                    }
747â†’                },

773â†’    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {

 51â†’/**
 52â†’ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * ```
 55â†’ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56â†’ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()
 57â†’ *
 58â†’ * @Test
 59â†’ * fun `Some test`() {
 60â†’ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61â†’ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 62â†’ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 63â†’ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")
 64â†’ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65â†’ * }
 66â†’ * ```
 67â†’ */
 68â†’@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69â†’public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70â†’    /**
 71â†’     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 72â†’     */
 73â†’    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
 74â†’    /**
 75â†’     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 76â†’     */

 78â†’    /**

 80â†’     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 81â†’     */
 82â†’    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
 83â†’    /**

 85â†’     */

 87â†’) {
 88â†’    private var filePath: String? = null
 89â†’    private var kotlinScript = false
 90â†’
 91â†’    /**
 92â†’     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
 93â†’     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94â†’     */
 95â†’    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96â†’        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97â†’
 98â†’        return this
 99â†’    }
100â†’
101â†’    /**
102â†’     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103â†’     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104â†’     * silently overwritten.
105â†’     *
106â†’     * Example of usage:
107â†’     * ```
108â†’     *  val code =
109â†’     *      """
110â†’     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111â†’     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112â†’     *      """.trimIndent()
113â†’     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114â†’     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115â†’     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116â†’     * ```
117â†’     */
118â†’    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119â†’    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120â†’        code
121â†’            .split("\n")
122â†’            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123â†’            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124â†’            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }
125â†’            ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
126â†’
127â†’        return this
128â†’    }
129â†’
130â†’    /**
131â†’     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
132â†’     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
133â†’     */
134â†’    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
135â†’        this.filePath = filePath
136â†’        return this
137â†’    }
138â†’
139â†’    /**
140â†’     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
141â†’     */
142â†’    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
143â†’        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
144â†’        return this
145â†’    }

147â†’    /**
148â†’     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
149â†’     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
150â†’     * indenting.
151â†’     *
152â†’     * Caution:
153â†’     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
154â†’     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
155â†’     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
156â†’     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
157â†’     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
158â†’     *
159â†’     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
160â†’     */
161â†’    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
162â†’        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
163â†’

177â†’     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
178â†’     *
179â†’     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProvider] when only a singe provider of a rule is to be added.
180â†’     */

191â†’     *

193â†’     */
194â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()
195â†’
196â†’    /**
197â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
198â†’     */
199â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
200â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
201â†’
202â†’    /**
203â†’     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
204â†’     *
205â†’     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
206â†’     */
207â†’    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
208â†’        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
209â†’
210â†’    /**
211â†’     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
212â†’     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
213â†’     */
214â†’    public fun hasLintViolation(
215â†’        line: Int,

1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’### Removed
 10â†’
 11â†’### Fixed
 12â†’
 13â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 14â†’
 15â†’### Changed
 16â†’
 17â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 18â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.3
 19â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 20â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.21` and Kotlin version to `1.8.21`.
 21â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 22â†’
 23â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 24â†’
 25â†’### Added
 26â†’
 27â†’### Removed
 28â†’
 29â†’### Fixed
 30â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
 31â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
 32â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
 33â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
 34â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
 35â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
 36â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
 37â†’* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
 38â†’* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
 39â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
 40â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
 41â†’
 42â†’### Changed
 43â†’
 44â†’* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
 45â†’
 46â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
 47â†’
 48â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
 49â†’
 50â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 51â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 52â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 53â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 54â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 55â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 56â†’
 57â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 58â†’
 59â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 60â†’
 61â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 62â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
 63â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
 64â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 65â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 66â†’
 67â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
 68â†’
 69â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
 70â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
 71â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
 72â†’* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
 73â†’* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
 74â†’* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
 75â†’* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
 76â†’* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
 77â†’* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
 78â†’* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
 79â†’* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
 80â†’* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
 81â†’* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
 82â†’
 83â†’Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
 84â†’
 85â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 86â†’
 87â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
 88â†’
 89â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
 90â†’
 91â†’#### Experimental rules
 92â†’
 93â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
 94â†’
 95â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 96â†’
 97â†’#### EditorConfig
 98â†’
 99â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
100â†’
101â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
102â†’
103â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
104â†’
105â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
106â†’
107â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
108â†’
109â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
110â†’
111â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
112â†’
113â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
114â†’
115â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
116â†’
117â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
118â†’
119â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
120â†’

        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
            functionBodyExpressionNodes

content.thinking:
**Summarizing final results and next steps**

Iâ€™m putting together a concise summary highlighting changes to FunctionSignatureRule, test additions, and changelog updates. Iâ€™ll note that `read_lints` was run on Kotlin files successfully but produced errors on the changelog since it's not Kotlin; those errors were expected and safely ignored. Iâ€™ll also mention tests run and wrap up without referencing any git commands.

content.text:
Summary:
- Updated `FunctionSignatureRule` to detect when a body expression starts with an annotation and avoid merging it onto the function signature line, preventing downstream violations in the `annotation` rule. Added a reusable `hasParentOfType` helper to make this check robust.
- Added a regression test (`FunctionSignatureRuleAnnotatedExpressionTest`) that reproduces the reported scenario to ensure annotated expression bodies stay on their own line.
- Documented the fix in `CHANGELOG.md` under the â€śFixedâ€ť section.

Tests:
- `./gradlew ktlint-ruleset-standard:test --tests com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.FunctionSignatureRuleAnnotatedExpressionTest`

(While running read_lints on the Kotlin files showed no issues, invoking it on `CHANGELOG.md` produced parse errors because the tool expects Kotlin sources; the markdown change itself is purely textual.)

 79â†’        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 80â†’        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]

 89â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90â†’        node: ASTNode,
 91â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 95â†’            node
 96â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
 97â†’                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 98â†’                .ifTrue {
 99â†’                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
100â†’                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
101â†’                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
102â†’                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
103â†’                    // effort to attempt it.
104â†’                    return
105â†’                }
106â†’
107â†’            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
108â†’        }
109â†’    }

111â†’    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
112â†’        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
113â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
114â†’        // or
115â†’        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
116â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
117â†’        val startOfBodyBlock =
118â†’            this
119â†’                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
120â†’                ?.firstChildNode
121â†’        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
122â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
123â†’            .childrenBetween(
124â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
125â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
126â†’            )
127â†’    }

130â†’        val funNode =

132â†’                this.treeParent
133â†’            } else {
134â†’                this
135â†’            }
136â†’        funNode
137â†’            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
138â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->

140â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
141â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
142â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
143â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&

146â†’                    ) {

155â†’    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
156â†’        node: ASTNode,
157â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
158â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
159â†’    ) {
160â†’        require(node.elementType == FUN)
161â†’
162â†’        val forceMultilineSignature =
163â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
164â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
165â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
166â†’        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
167â†’            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
168â†’            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
169â†’            // like below, which are not acceptable:
170â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
171â†’            //     ) = "some-value"
172â†’            //
173â†’            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
174â†’            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
175â†’            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
176â†’            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
177â†’            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
178â†’            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
179â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
180â†’                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
181â†’                // signature needs to be recalculated
182â†’                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
183â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
184â†’            } else {
185â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
186â†’                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
187â†’            }
188â†’        } else {
189â†’            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
190â†’            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
191â†’            // signature.
192â†’            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
193â†’                node
194â†’                    .functionSignatureNodes()
195â†’                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
196â†’            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
197â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
198â†’            } else {
199â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
200â†’            }
201â†’        }
202â†’    }

204â†’    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
205â†’        val closingParenthesis =
206â†’            node
207â†’                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
208â†’                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
209â†’        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
210â†’            node
211â†’                .functionSignatureNodes()
212â†’                .childrenBetween(
213â†’                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
214â†’                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
215â†’                )
216â†’
217â†’        return node.indent(false).length +
218â†’            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
219â†’    }

241â†’    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
242â†’        node: ASTNode,
243â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
244â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
247â†’
248â†’        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
249â†’        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
250â†’        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
251â†’            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
252â†’            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
253â†’    }

262â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
263â†’        node: ASTNode,
264â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
265â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
266â†’        multiline: Boolean,
267â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
268â†’    ): Int {
269â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
270â†’
271â†’        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
272â†’        val firstParameterInList =
273â†’            valueParameterList
274â†’                .children()
275â†’                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
276â†’
277â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
278â†’            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
279â†’                // handle empty parameter list
280â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
281â†’            } else {
282â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
283â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
285â†’            }
286â†’
287â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
288â†’    }

290â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
291â†’        node: ASTNode,
292â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
293â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
294â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
295â†’    ): Int {

299â†’

301â†’            .children()
302â†’            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
303â†’            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
304â†’                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
305â†’                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
306â†’                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
307â†’            }
308â†’            .firstOrNull()
309â†’            ?.let { whiteSpace ->

322â†’            }

674â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
675â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
676â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
677â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
678â†’        val iterator = iterator()
679â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
680â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
681â†’
682â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
683â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
684â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
685â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
686â†’                break
687â†’            }
688â†’        }
689â†’
690â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
692â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
693â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694â†’                break
695â†’            }
696â†’        }
697â†’
698â†’        return childrenBetween
699â†’    }

 510â†’    // The FunctionSignatureWrappingRule depends on a lot of different rules to do an initial clean up. The tests below
 511â†’    // ensure that those rules effectively clean up so that the FunctionSignatureWrappingRule does not need to check for
 512â†’    // it at all.
 513â†’    @Nested
 514â†’    inner class CleanUpByRelatedRules {
 515â†’        @Test
 516â†’        fun `Given a nullable type with a space before the quest then remove this space`() {
 517â†’            val code =
 518â†’                """
 519â†’                fun String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES?.f1() = "some-result"
 520â†’                fun List<String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES?>.f2() = "some-result"
 521â†’                fun f3(string: String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES?) = "some-result"
 522â†’                fun f4(string: List<String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES?>) = "some-result"
 523â†’                fun f5(): String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES? = "some-result"
 524â†’                fun f6(): List<String$UNEXPECTED_SPACES?> = listOf("some-result", null)
 525â†’                fun f7(): List<String>$UNEXPECTED_SPACES? = null
 526â†’                """.trimIndent() // ktlint-disable string-template
 527â†’            val formattedCode =
 528â†’                """
 529â†’                fun String?.f1() = "some-result"
 530â†’                fun List<String?>.f2() = "some-result"
 531â†’                fun f3(string: String?) = "some-result"
 532â†’                fun f4(string: List<String?>) = "some-result"
 533â†’                fun f5(): String? = "some-result"
 534â†’                fun f6(): List<String?> = listOf("some-result", null)
 535â†’                fun f7(): List<String>? = null
 536â†’                """.trimIndent()
 537â†’            functionSignatureWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 538â†’                .addAdditionalRuleProvider { NullableTypeSpacingRule() }
 539â†’                .hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
 540â†’                .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 541â†’        }

 543â†’        @Test
 544â†’        fun `Given a function signature contains redundant spaces then ensure that those are removed before running the function signature rule`() {
 545â†’            val code =
 546â†’                """
